questions.question: Is the disabled/enabled state of the 'Request to Join' button genuinely derived from `hasShareableName(profile.nickname)` re-evaluated on every relevant state change (keystroke, profile update), rather than a frozen boolean computed once at mount, a hardcoded `disabled` prop that never flips, or a check against a stale closed-over `profile` snapshot? Provide the component code path from input `onChange` to the button's `disabled` prop, and a test that types a name then asserts the button becomes enabled without any remount or prop-forced re-render.

Does the diff confine itself to this story's declared scope — `JoinRequestCard.tsx` (Join card UI module) and new/edited keys in `app/src/lib/i18n.ts` — with no new cross-module import beyond the two exports architecture.md's Boundary rules permit (`saveProfile` from `ProfileContext`, `hasShareableName` from `shareCard.ts`)? Run `grep -n '^import' app/src/components/groups/JoinRequestCard.tsx` and confirm every import resolves to an existing, already-permitted source — no new import of `joinRequestSender.ts`, `welcomeSubscription.ts`, or any other module this epic touches.

Could 'Join card UI' (`app/src/components/groups/JoinRequestCard.tsx`) be deleted and rewritten without modifying any file outside its dependencies_allowed list — here, the existing exports `saveProfile` (Profile state) and `hasShareableName` (Name predicate), per architecture.md's Module map and Boundary rules? If not, which cross-module dependency exists (e.g. a change forced onto `ProfileContext.tsx` beyond calling the existing `saveProfile` signature, or a new field added to `hasShareableName`'s contract), and does it flow through those declared exports or does it reach into either module's internals?

Given this repo has no jsdom / component-render / snapshot capability (vitest only — see project testing conventions and AC-GATE-4's explicit note), which parts of the gate logic were extracted into a plain, unit-testable function or hook (e.g. a pure `shouldGateJoin(profile, guardsPassed)`-style predicate) versus which parts rely exclusively on the e2e suite (AC-ETE-1, AC-ETE-2) for verification? List the specific unit test(s), if any, and confirm the render-order and reactivity claims (AC-GATE-3, AC-GATE-6, AC-GATE-7) are NOT silently left unverified by assuming e2e coverage that doesn't yet exist until S5 lands.

Does this story avoid the two behaviors architecture.md and the spec explicitly exclude — no redirect to `/profile` (DD-2; the field renders in place on the join card) and no nickname write path other than `saveProfile`? Run `grep -n 'router.push\|router.replace\|/profile' app/src/components/groups/JoinRequestCard.tsx` and confirm no navigation to a profile route is introduced, and `grep -n 'nickname' app/src/components/groups/JoinRequestCard.tsx` to confirm the only write call is `saveProfile(...)`.

Is the 'disabled' state of the Request-to-Join button actually inert against alternate triggers — e.g. does pressing Enter inside the name input, or a form `onSubmit` handler, bypass the disabled button and still invoke `sendJoinRequest`? Provide a test that focuses the name input while nameless, presses Enter, and asserts `sendJoinRequest` was NOT called (not merely that the button's `disabled` attribute is true).

When a nameless user (`hasShareableName(profile.nickname)` is false) opens a valid invite link, does `JoinRequestCard` render a name `<Input>` AND render 'Request to Join' in a disabled state, and does clicking that disabled button provably NOT call `sendJoinRequest`? Provide the render output/DOM assertion (via e2e, given no component-render capability) and a click-attempt test asserting the send function's call count stays zero.

Does the group name (from the link's `name` query param) remain visible on the card at the same time the name input is shown, for every nameless-user render of this card — i.e. is there any code path where the name input renders but the group-name element is conditionally hidden or removed?

Is the gate reactive rather than evaluated once on mount: does entering a name that satisfies `hasShareableName` enable the button live (no remount), and does clearing that name back to a nameless value disable the button again live, in the same mounted instance? Provide a test performing type-then-assert-enabled, then clear-then-assert-disabled-again as two sequential assertions against one mounted component/page, not two separate fresh loads.

When a user who already has a name (`hasShareableName` true) opens the same link, does the card render exactly as it does today — no name input, no helper text, button enabled — with zero code-path difference from the pre-epic render for this case? Since no snapshot capability exists, is this verified via AC-ETE-2 (existing named-user e2e spec, unmodified) PLUS an explicit new e2e assertion that no name input element is present for that case, rather than left as an unverified assumption?

Does submitting the name call `saveProfile` (`ProfileContext.tsx:52-58`) exclusively — no other write path to the nickname field — and is the submitted name subsequently readable on `/profile` as the user's real nickname (not a per-request alias stored elsewhere)? Provide the call site and a test asserting `readStoredContacts`/profile-read state reflects the submitted name after a submit-then-navigate-to-profile sequence.

Is the name gate evaluated only AFTER the existing hydration/identity guard (`JoinRequestCard.tsx:43-59`) and the invalid-admin-npub guard (`:61-70`) — never before them — such that a user with an unhydrated identity still sees the existing 'setting up…' state and never the name input? Provide the exact conditional ordering in the diff (line numbers) and a test that simulates an unhydrated-identity state and asserts the name input is absent while the setting-up state is present.

Does the name gate leave the already-a-member path (`JoinRequestCard.tsx:39-41`) completely unaffected, such that a nameless user who is already a member sees the existing already-member state rather than a name prompt? Provide a test with a nameless-but-already-member fixture asserting the name input never renders and the already-member UI renders as it does today.

Does every new user-facing string this story introduces (field label, helper text, disabled-button state) have both `en` and `de` entries in `app/src/lib/i18n.ts`, consumed exclusively via `useCopy()`, with zero hardcoded string literals in `JoinRequestCard.tsx`? Run `grep -n '"[A-Z][a-z]' app/src/components/groups/JoinRequestCard.tsx` scoped to JSX text nodes and confirm no new hardcoded user-facing string appears outside a `copy.*` reference.

Do the new i18n keys follow the existing `copy.groups.joinRequest*` grouping and naming convention (per `app/src/lib/i18n.ts:155-161` en / `:775-782` de-adjacent), rather than introducing a new top-level or inconsistently-named group? Provide the new key names and confirm each is nested under `copy.groups.joinRequest`.

The implementation calls `useProfile()`, `useState`, and the `useEffect` that syncs `nameDraft` from `profile.nickname` unconditionally at the top of the component -- BEFORE the hydration/identity/invalid-admin-npub/already-member early returns (`JoinRequestCard.tsx:43-112`) -- because React's Rules of Hooks forbid calling hooks after a conditional return. `gateActive`/`requestDisabled` are computed at that same point but are inert (unused) until the final JSX return that those guards gate. Confirm this hook-ordering is in fact required (not incidental), and that no *visible* gate behavior (the rendered name input, the button's disabled state) can reach the user before the pre-existing guards resolve -- i.e. the computation happening early is a React-mechanics necessity, not a violation of AC-GATE-6/7's intent.

`handleSendRequest` calls `saveProfile({ ...profile, nickname: nameDraft })` BEFORE attempting `sendJoinRequest`, and does not roll it back if the subsequent send throws (the existing `catch` only sets an error message and re-enables the button -- `nameDraft`'s already-persisted value is untouched). Confirm this is the intended reading of AC-GATE-5 ("submitting the name" persists the profile nickname unconditionally, independent of whether the join request itself succeeds) rather than an accidental side effect -- i.e. a user who enters a name, has the send fail, and retries should NOT be asked for a name a second time, and should NOT have the name silently discarded on failure.

`saveProfile` (called from the new gate-submit path) only updates local React state and `writeUserProfile`'s localStorage persistence (`ProfileContext.tsx:52-58`) -- it does NOT invoke `publishProfileUpdate` or any MLS/group broadcast, unlike the profile page's own nickname-save path which separately calls `broadcastProfile`. Confirm that `JoinRequestCard.tsx` never imports or calls `publishProfileUpdate`/`broadcastProfile`/any fan-out helper, so a nameless invitee's freshly-entered name reaches the admin ONLY inside the existing gift-wrapped join-request rumor (once S2 lands), never via a public or group-wide profile broadcast triggered by this save.
